document.name: Alley Pond Environmental Center — Early Childhood Nature Classes
document.shortDescription: Queens nature center in Alley Pond Park: caregiver-and-me and drop-off early childhood classes with live animals, nature walks, stories, and science-themed pre-K readiness.
document.longDescription:
Alley Pond Environmental Center (APEC) is a nature education center in Alley Pond Park at 229-10 Northern Boulevard in Douglaston, Queens. Its official early childhood programs page lists ongoing classes for ages 18 months through 4 years, including Wee Sprouts (18–23 months with one adult), Toddler Time (ages 2–3 with one adult), Lil' Hoppers (ages 3–4 with one adult), and Sunny Bunnies (ages 3–4 drop-off pre-K readiness with live animals, nature walks, and school-readiness skills).

The homepage notes the building and animal room are open most weekdays 9:00 a.m.–4:30 p.m. and most weekends 9:00 a.m.–1:00 p.m. (call ahead on weekends and holidays), with free parking and a suggested $5 per person donation at the front desk. Example session pricing on the early childhood page includes Toddler Time blocks such as four Saturday sessions for $88 per child/adult pair and Sunny Bunnies spring semesters listed at $990 for 30 classes; pre-registration is required via APEC's online registration system.

Main telephone published on the site: (718) 229-4000. APEC directs questions through its official contact form and notes it is not a licensed wildlife rehabilitator.

This listing image is an official photograph published on alleypond.org, re-hosted on ImgBB for ClassScout storage policy compliance.

Sources: https://www.alleypond.org/ https://www.alleypond.org/early-childhood-programs.html https://www.alleypond.org/contact.html
document.announcementDescription: Official early childhood page lists 2026 Sunny Bunnies, Toddler Time, Wee Sprouts, and Lil' Hoppers sessions with links to alleypond.doubleknot.com registration.
